exec_args.query: SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table';

args.query: SELECT "Index", SUM(CASE WHEN Close>Open THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days, SUM(CASE WHEN Close<Open THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_days, SUM(CASE WHEN Close=Open THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS flat_days, COUNT(*) AS total_days FROM index_trade WHERE "Index" IN ('IXIC','NYA','GSPTSE') AND Date BETWEEN '2018-01-01' AND '2018-12-31' GROUP BY "Index";

exec_args.sql: SELECT "Index", SUM(CASE WHEN Close>Open THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days, SUM(CASE WHEN Close<Open THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_days, SUM(CASE WHEN Close=Open THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS flat_days, COUNT(*) AS total_days FROM index_trade WHERE "Index" IN ('IXIC','NYA','GSPTSE') AND Date BETWEEN '2018-01-01' AND '2018-12-31' GROUP BY "Index";

result.preview:
"The North American indices with more up than down days in 2018 were:

- IXIC (NASDAQ Composite): 44 up days vs 31 down days
- GSPTSE (S&P/TSX Composite): 44 up days vs 41 down days

(NYA — NYSE Composite — had 36 up days vs 42 down days.)"

args.answer:
The North American indices with more up than down days in 2018 were:

- IXIC (NASDAQ Composite): 44 up days vs 31 down days
- GSPTSE (S&P/TSX Composite): 44 up days vs 41 down days

(NYA — NYSE Composite — had 36 up days vs 42 down days.)
